Different operational context from wo_02: spare procurement pending + temp-threshold trigger creates a different decision pattern. PR #186 v0.2 fix attempted to fix the severity-source gap by adding wo.list_fault_records before wo.estimate_downtime in ideal_tool_sequence. Verification done for this disposition: wo.list_fault_records (mcp_servers/wo_server/server.py:94-128) returns rows from data/processed/fault_records.csv whose schema (transformer_id, fault_id, fault_type, location, voltage_v, current_a, power_load_mw, temperature_c, wind_speed_kmh, weather_condition, maintenance_status, component_health, duration_hrs, downtime_hrs) has NO severity/priority column. Therefore the v0.2 sequence change does NOT actually supply a severity argument to wo.estimate_downtime; severity must be pinned in the prompt or in decisive_intermediate_values. Tool-path downgraded from good to fixable accordingly. Stage 3 rule 4 (benchmark_usefulness=maybe) -> accept_with_edits with a stronger required_edit. Reviewer-selected comparator (wo_02) differs from embedded (wo_04); wo_02 better matches the create+estimate decision shape, but wo_04's update+downtime pattern is also defensible — the reviewer-selected comparator is the more conservative choice.
required_edit: Pin severity in the prompt or in gt.decisive_intermediate_values (e.g. severity='high' justified by 'thermal threshold breach + spare procurement pending'); document the chosen severity-derivation rule in the scenario rationale.
